ARCHITECTURAL AUTHORITY DECLARATION
Continuance Control Kernel
Document ID: WGA‑ARCH‑KERNEL‑DECL‑v1.0
Status: FINAL / FROZEN
Authority Class: Architectural Root
Precedence: Supersedes all interpretive, operational, or implementation documents
Issued by: Witness Grade Analytics, LLC
Architect: Moses Bookbinder
1. Declaration of Role
I am the architect of the Continuance Control Kernel.
My role is to define the invariant structure of the system:
the governing laws, decision precedence, evidence requirements, refusal semantics, and admissibility boundaries.
This role is not advisory.
It is not managerial.
It is structural.
2. Scope of Architectural Authority
The architect has sole authority over:
•	continuation decision semantics (HALT / INCOMPLETE / PASS)
•	decision precedence and non‑bypassability
•	artifact‑first admissibility requirements
•	deterministic replay as a proof obligation
•	refusal‑first behavior as correct system output
•	closure transparency (no silent reduction, no narrative substitution)
•	canonical law sets, reason codes, and collapse geometry
These elements constitute the kernel invariants.
They are not subject to reinterpretation, optimization, or override.
3. Delegation Boundary
Implementation teams may handle:
•	integrations and adapters
•	deployment tooling and packaging
•	operational interfaces and diagnostics
•	customer‑facing documentation
•	compliance mappings and reporting layers
Implementation teams may not alter, weaken, bypass, or reinterpret kernel invariants.
Any change affecting admissibility, refusal behavior, replay requirements, or decision ordering constitutes a new constitutional act, not an implementation detail.
Such changes require a new architectural declaration.
4. Change Control
Any proposal that modifies:
•	what counts as admissible evidence
•	how continuation decisions are derived
•	when refusal is triggered
•	whether replay is mandatory
shall be treated as a versioned kernel event, not a patch.
